description_long: This barn features a tall, steeply pitched roof with a gentle curve, reminiscent of traditional European or English rural architecture. The exterior showcases a timber frame structure with visible vertical and horizontal wooden beams, creating a grid pattern on the walls. The main entrance is a single, centrally located door with a small, square window or vent above it, and no other windows are visible on any side. The roof is expansive and overhangs the walls, providing additional protection from the elements. The siding between the timber beams appears to be infilled with a lighter material, possibly plaster or wattle and daub, giving a classic half-timbered appearance. The overall form is compact and functional, suitable for agricultural storage or shelter.
description: A tall, half-timbered barn with a steeply curved roof and a single central door, featuring a traditional timber frame and infilled wall panels.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long, curved roof and the regular spacing of vertical timber beams along the wall.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance door, timber framing, and the symmetrical gable end of the roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the continuous roof and timber structure without any doors or windows.
description_view_3: Rear view mirroring the front, but without a door or any visible openings, showing the full timber frame pattern.
attribute_door_style: single hinged door, centrally located, rectangular with a small vent or window above
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows except a small vent or window above the main door
